description: Define the hard-cutover invariant that recurring invoice execution identity is service-period/execution-window based only and never requires `billing_cycle_id`.
Document the retained role of `client_billing_cycles` as cadence/source-rule infrastructure and optional historical metadata only.
Remove mixed-schema missing-table/missing-column guards from recurring invoice code paths.
Remove compatibility due-work synthesis from `client_billing_cycles` for recurring invoice-ready rows.
Remove `missing_service_period_materialization` fallback behavior that fabricates recurring compatibility rows instead of surfacing a repairable failure.
Make the recurring due-work reader source all ready recurring work exclusively from `recurring_service_periods`.
Represent client-cadence recurring due work from canonical recurring service-period records without requiring a `billing_cycle_id` bridge.
Remove bridge-only fields from recurring due-work row identity contracts where they are still required for execution.
Retain optional billing-cycle context on recurring due-work rows only as passive display metadata if still useful.
Remove recurring run-target builders that start from `getAvailableBillingPeriods(...)` or raw cycle IDs.
Make recurring batch/run target selection operate only on canonical recurring due-work rows.
Make recurring job payloads use canonical execution-window or service-period identity only.
Remove recurring job-handler fallback logic that reconstructs live recurring work from `billing_cycle_id`.
Remove `billingCycleId` as a required field from shared recurring execution identity contracts.
Redefine client-cadence recurring execution identity using canonical schedule/window/service-period identity rather than cycle UUIDs.
Remove bridge-vs-execution-window branching from recurring identity helpers.
Remove legacy recurring comparison-mode branches that only exist to preserve bridge-era behavior.
Make recurring duplicate detection key off canonical execution-window/service-period identity and linked recurring rows only.
Stop preferring `invoices.billing_cycle_id` as the duplicate key for recurring client-cadence invoices.
Ensure rerun/retry behavior for recurring invoices is driven by canonical recurring identity rather than invoice header bridge fields.
Remove recurring preview request shapes that accept `billing_cycle_id` as a first-class recurring input.
Remove recurring generate request shapes that accept `billing_cycle_id` as a first-class recurring input.
Make recurring preview browser actions use canonical selector input only.
Make recurring generate browser actions use canonical selector input only.
Remove recurring API/controller naming and semantics that still frame preview/generate around billing cycles.
Remove recurring API compatibility wrappers that preserve `billing_cycle_id` as a recurring contract.
Keep or introduce separate non-recurring/client-cycle APIs where legitimate client billing schedule operations still need them.
Update shared recurring timing interfaces to remove bridge-only fields such as required `billingCycleId` and `hasBillingCycleBridge`.
Update shared recurring due-selection interfaces to make recurring identity fully canonical and bridge-free.
Update invoice preview/generation error contracts so recurring diagnostics do not depend on `billingCycleId`.
Remove recurring invoice DTO semantics that classify or explain recurring behavior through `billing_cycle_id`.
Preserve `billing_cycle_id` on invoice DTOs only as optional historical/client-context metadata if still needed.
Make invoice list/detail/read models derive recurring semantics from canonical recurring summary/detail data instead of `invoices.billing_cycle_id`.
Remove recurring invoice classification branches that infer cadence or execution-window kind from billing-cycle bridge presence.
Make recurring invoice detail rows canonical-first, using linked recurring detail/service-period data instead of bridge-era projection layers.
Remove or collapse `recurring_projection`-style compatibility layers where they only preserve bridge-shaped recurring reads.
Make recurring history queries invoice/service-period based rather than billing-cycle based.
Remove recurring history APIs or action names that present recurring invoices as invoiced billing cycles.
Make recurring reverse/delete flows operate on invoice-linked recurring service-period records, not billing-cycle handles.
Remove recurring reversal logic that branches on whether `billingCycleId` is present to choose the primary object to mutate.
Repair recurring service-period lifecycle state after reverse/delete using canonical linkage only.
Make recurring invoice-to-service-period linkage canonical and bridge-independent for all cadence owners.
Remove invoice-linkage matching rules that widen or narrow based on `invoice.billing_cycle_id` presence.
Remove recurring invoice-linkage schema-probing fallback logic that is only needed for mixed recurring schemas.
Ensure recurring charge/detail persistence carries enough canonical obligation and period identity to repair linkage without a billing-cycle bridge.
Reclassify invoice kinds explicitly so prepayment and similar behaviors no longer use null/non-null `billing_cycle_id` as a proxy.
Fix invoice finalization/modification logic that misclassifies bridge-less recurring invoices as prepayments.
Keep manual/prepayment invoice behavior correct after recurring bridge removal.
Remove recurring use of `client_billing_cycles` inside the billing engine as a source of recurring truth.
Keep client cadence source-rule resolution in the billing engine only as input to recurring service-period materialization.
Remove recurring engine paths that auto-create or load billing-cycle rows to execute recurring work.
Validate recurring invoice windows against canonical recurring service periods instead of cycle-row mutation semantics.
Make client billing schedule changes regenerate future recurring service periods instead of defining future recurring work by mutating future cycle rows.
Keep legitimate client billing schedule management flows for non-recurring/client-cadence administration.
Update bucket recurring period resolution to derive from canonical recurring service periods instead of preferring `client_billing_cycles`.
Make hourly and usage recurring execution continue to bill by canonical service window after bridge removal.
Remove recurring accounting export fallback provenance states that preserve canonical-vs-header bridge period logic.
Make accounting export use canonical recurring detail/service-period data only for recurring invoices.
Treat invoices lacking canonical recurring detail as non-recurring/periodless in export paths instead of preserving recurring fallback provenance.
Remove recurring UI row-action requirements that depend on `billing_cycle_id` existence.
Update recurring UI copy to describe service-period/execution-window objects directly instead of billing cycles.
Keep explicit UX defaults for `cadence_owner` only as product defaults, not as runtime fallback semantics.
Remove rollout-era authoring helpers that still resolve unspecified recurring cadence to implicit client fallback behavior.
Remove recurring metadata compatibility shims in authoring/storage helpers where old-table fallback is no longer supported.
Retain canonical recurring service-period inspection/management surfaces as the operator tool for recurring troubleshooting.
Turn missing recurring materialization into explicit operator-visible repair actions rather than compatibility due-work rows.
Document and enforce that recurring service-period structures are required schema, not optional rollout-era schema.
Remove recurring code comments and runbook language that still present the billing-cycle bridge as a normal recurring model.
Update recurring workflow/event diagnostics to use canonical recurring execution identity only.
Remove diagnostic/event payload fields whose only purpose is to preserve recurring billing-cycle bridge semantics.
Audit and clean invoice queries so recurring invoice period summaries do not rely on billing-cycle bridge assumptions.
Audit and clean API schemas so only legitimate client billing schedule endpoints still expose billing-cycle-specific recurring request shapes.
Audit and clean package/shared interfaces so client billing cycle models are separated from recurring execution models.
Define a deprecation posture for `invoices.billing_cycle_id` in recurring code, including whether it remains passive metadata or has a later removal path.
Add a migration/read-side strategy for historically incomplete recurring linkage so live recurring logic can still drop the bridge assumptions.
Remove billing-cycle-specific recurring tests that only preserve replaced behaviors.
Add canonical recurring tests proving client-cadence recurring invoicing works entirely through service periods.
Add canonical recurring tests proving contract-cadence recurring invoicing remains bridge-free end to end.
Add regression coverage proving explicit invoice kind classification prevents bridge-less recurring invoices from being misclassified as prepayments.
Add hard-cutover documentation and runbook guidance describing the final recurring model and the removed bridge assumptions.
